displaced.title: you signed in on another device.
displaced.body: tameru is active there now. take over on this device, or sign in again.

common.autoLoggedTooltip: auto-logged by tameru — detected as a recurring charge.

philosophy.title: why manual?
philosophy.p1: Most spending apps sync your bank automatically. Tameru doesn't — on purpose.
philosophy.p2: The act of logging a purchase, even for 10 seconds, is what builds awareness. Mint synced everything automatically. People still overspent, because automatic means invisible.
philosophy.p3: Tameru asks you to log what you spend. The AI handles the rest — categorization, patterns, questions, nudges. You bring the data. Tameru brings the intelligence.
philosophy.p4: If that sounds like the right trade, let's get started.

notifications.title: notifications
notifications.subtitle: tameru speaks softly. you choose how often.

signOut.title: sign out?
signOut.body: your data stays exactly where it is. you can sign back in anytime — nothing is deleted.

privacy.title: privacy
privacy.subtitle: what tameru does with your data, and what it doesn't.

analytics.label: pause product analytics
analytics.desc: stops feature-usage events immediately. transaction data is never sent to analytics.

export.label: export my data
export.desc: downloads a json file with your transactions, cards, subscriptions, memory facts, chat history, and preferences.
export.errorRetry: couldn't prepare your export. try again in a moment.
export.errorGeneric: something went wrong. try again in a moment.

deleteAccount.label: delete my account
deleteAccount.desc: email us and we'll wipe everything within 7 days. an in-app button ships in a later release.
deleteAccount.emailBodyLine2: Please delete my account and all associated data.
deleteAccount.emailBodyLine3: I understand this is permanent and includes my transactions, cards, subscriptions, chat history, and memory facts.

disclosure.aiProvidersAnthropicParagraph: when you chat with tameru — to add a transaction, to ask a question, or anything else — your message goes to anthropic. card multiplier lookups also go to anthropic and include only the public card name and last 4 digits, never your transaction data. anthropic's default retention is 30 days for trust & safety review; zero data retention has been requested for the tameru org and brings that to zero. anthropic does not use api data for training under any tier.
disclosure.aiProvidersGeminiParagraph: we also use google gemini on its paid tier to pick a category for each transaction — for that, only the merchant name and amount are sent. when you scan a receipt, the photo itself goes to gemini (it can show line items, a store address, or a partial card number); it's used only to read the merchant, total, and date, and the image isn't stored. gemini parses csv imports the same way. google's paid tier does not use api data for training; the free tier does, and we never use it.
disclosure.analyticsParagraph1: we use posthog (us cloud) to count structural events only: chat-session starts and ends, feature usage, onboarding steps, the weekly digest opens, and shown errors. we never send transaction amounts, merchant names, card details, or the text of what you ask in chat.
disclosure.analyticsParagraph2: the toggle above stops new events immediately. anything we already counted stays in posthog until you ask us to remove it.

subtitle.before: apps you've allowed to read your spending — like

unsubscribe.title: unsubscribe from the weekly digest?
unsubscribe.body: tameru will stop sending the monday digest email to your inbox. you can re-enable it anytime in settings → notifications.
unsubscribe.doneBody: tameru won't send you weekly digest emails anymore. you can re-enable them anytime in settings → notifications inside the app.
unsubscribe.invalidBody: the unsubscribe link is missing its token. use the link from your most recent digest email.
unsubscribe.errorBody: something went wrong. try again, or turn the digest off in settings → notifications.
